Z38
[tsoyg-nis] — testimony, witness

Bearing witness
through animation

Named for the German word Zeugnis — testimony, witness — and the 38th parallel that divides Korea, our studio carries forward the legacy of the Gesellschaft für Menschenrechte, the West German organization that meticulously documented East German defector testimonies to build cases for accountability. We apply that same discipline to animation: transforming firsthand accounts of refugees and survivors into films that serve as both powerful storytelling and immutable evidence.

Our Mission

The stories no one
thinks to tell

The world does not lack for suffering. What it lacks is attention. North Korean defectors, Syrian refugees, trafficked women crossing borders they never chose — their experiences unfold in places most audiences will never visit, in countries that rarely command global sympathy. Testimony alone does not reach people. Reports gather dust. Even documentary film, for all its power, struggles to find audiences for subjects the world has decided to look away from.

We believe something different. We believe people are drawn to human stories — stories about families, love, survival, and hope — regardless of where those stories originate. A mother holding her child in a snowstorm outside a detention center is not a North Korean story. It is a human one. The challenge has never been whether these stories matter. It has been finding a medium powerful enough, and accessible enough, to carry them to a global audience that would otherwise never encounter them.

Animation is that medium. It crosses language barriers. It protects the identities of those still at risk. It renders events no camera witnessed. And it invites audiences in — including those who might instinctively turn away from live-action footage of human suffering — because the form itself signals that this is, above all, a story worth hearing. Behind every film, we build a meticulous evidentiary record: testimony transcripts, corroborating documentation, and blockchain-secured metadata assembled to meet the standards of international legal proceedings. Our films reveal the human condition. Our archives hold perpetrators to account.

Award-Winning Debut

Freedom's Last Embrace:
A North Korean Accordion Boy

Our inaugural film, winner of the 2024 International Creative Competition (Film Category), follows a young boy and his family through escape, trafficking, detention, and the long passage toward asylum.

Scene 1
A North Korean apartment block, its facade emblazoned with state propaganda — the only reality the boy has ever known.
Scene 2
An accordion classroom under the watchful eye of a stern instructor — music as state discipline, childhood as indoctrination.
Scene 3
Children march beneath red flags while laborers toil in the fields — daily life under the regime's unyielding grip.
Scene 4
At a local inn, the boy meets his father for the journey that will change everything — a threshold between the known and the unknown.
Scene 5
Arrested by Chinese authorities — the family faces the threat of forced repatriation, a fate that could mean imprisonment or death.
Scene 6
Three months later — a mother holds her child close as snow falls outside their uncertain refuge, survival measured in days.

The animation follows a young accordion student and his family as they flee North Korea — first across the border into China, then through a gauntlet of trafficking networks, detention by Chinese authorities, and the ever-present threat of forced repatriation. The story is drawn from firsthand defector testimonies, rendered through a visual style that protects identities while conveying the weight of lived experience.

What sets this film apart is not only its subject matter but its method: each narrative element corresponds to documented testimony. The animation serves as both a creative work and a visual index of the evidentiary record behind it — a model for every film this studio will produce.

What We Do

Three pillars of our work

Every project at Zeugnis38Films moves through three integrated disciplines, each reinforcing the others.

01

Testimony & Documentation

Teams of trained interviewers, translators, and legal advisors gather firsthand accounts from refugees and survivors. Each testimony is recorded in structured formats that satisfy evidentiary standards — fixed-form questionnaires, video depositions, and corroborating materials — then digitized and secured on an immutable blockchain ledger.

Structured Interviews Legal Standards Blockchain Archive
02

Animated Storytelling

Our directors, writers, and artists transform documented testimonies into compelling films. Animation is uniquely suited to this work: it protects the identities of those still at risk, renders events that no camera witnessed, and delivers emotional truth across cultures and languages without exploiting graphic imagery.

Identity Protection Emotional Truth Global Reach
03

Distribution & Accountability

Completed films reach global audiences through streaming platforms, film festivals, educational institutions, and advocacy organizations. Simultaneously, the evidentiary archive grows — available to international tribunals, human rights courts, and truth commissions when justice becomes possible.

YouTube & Netflix Tribunals Education
Our Process

From testimony to screen

A rigorous pipeline ensures that every story is both artistically compelling and legally defensible.

I

Gather

Trained interviewers conduct structured sessions with refugees and survivors, capturing testimony in standardized legal and narrative formats.

II

Secure

Every record — transcripts, documents, multimedia — is digitized, time-stamped, and committed to an immutable blockchain ledger.

III

Animate

Directors and AI-assisted production tools transform verified testimonies into films, leveraging scalable workflows to produce at volume.

IV

Deliver

Films reach audiences via YouTube, Netflix, festivals, and educational networks — while the archive stands ready for courts and commissions.

Upcoming Projects

Stories in development

Each project begins with testimony — gathered, verified, and secured — before a single frame is drawn.

01

The Crossing

A series of short films documenting the trafficking networks that move North Korean women through China — told through the voices of survivors who made it to safety and those still searching for family members left behind.

North Korea In Development
02

Letters from Mariupol

Animated testimonies from Ukrainian civilians displaced by the siege of Mariupol — capturing the bombardment, the shelters, the evacuations, and the long displacement that followed, drawn from interviews conducted in Poland and Germany.

Ukraine Pre-Production
03

The Political Camps

First-person accounts from survivors of North Korea's political prison camps — forced labor, starvation, public executions, and the systematic erasure of families deemed politically disloyal. Animation as the only medium that can render what no camera entered.

North Korea Research Phase
04

Children of the Route

Stories from unaccompanied minors who crossed through Iran, Turkey, and the Balkans — the smugglers, the camps, the kindness of strangers, and the bureaucratic limbo of asylum systems that leaves children stateless for years.

Middle East Concept Phase
Global Reach

Where the stories are

North Korea
Founding Focus

Political imprisonment, forced labor, trafficking of women and girls through China, starvation, separated families, and the perilous escape routes that define the defector experience.

Ukraine & Eastern Europe
Active Documentation

Civilian displacement, war crimes, forced deportation of children, and the long-term toll of sustained bombardment on families and communities.

Middle East & Central Asia
Expanding Coverage

From Syria and Afghanistan to Iran and Gaza — documenting displacement, sectarian violence, the suppression of women's rights, and refugee corridors spanning continents.

Technology & Scale

Built for truth
at scale

Our ambition is not a single film but hundreds — an entire library of animated testimony spanning continents and decades. Achieving this demands infrastructure that traditional studios cannot offer.

Blockchain Evidence Chain

Every testimony, document, and media file is hashed and stored on a distributed ledger, creating a tamper-proof chain of custody admissible in international courts.

AI-Assisted Animation Pipeline

Generative AI accelerates storyboarding, background rendering, and motion workflows, enabling a distributed team to produce high-quality animation at a pace impossible by conventional means.

Distributed Production Model

A global network of volunteers, interns, and studio professionals collaborate through secure cloud pipelines — conducting interviews, translating testimony, producing animation, and managing distribution simultaneously across time zones.

Join the Work

These stories will not
tell themselves

Every film requires funding, expertise, and time. Whether you are a donor, a filmmaker, a legal professional, or someone who believes documented truth is the precondition of justice — there is a role for you here.
